applicable_regime.explanation: Accrual date 2026-01-28 ≥ 2023-03-24 → HB 837 modified-comparative 51% bar applies (§768.81(6)).

fact_pattern_anchor.controlling_authority: Birge v. Charron, 107 So. 3d 350 (Fla. 2012); Pierce v. Progressive Am. Ins. Co.; Eppler v. Tarmac

doctrine_gates_applied.effect: Modified-comparative 51% bar for non-medical-negligence accruing after 2023-03-24.
Rear driver presumed at fault unless four named rebuttals evidenced.
doctrine_gates_applied.statute_or_case_cite: Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert

rationale.net_apportionment_walk:
Start: rear_end anchor = 95% (rear_driver → insured_vanguard_driver).
  insured_more_fault: 15 pts (police_report_narrative (party_admission) — DOC-007-CHP)
  insured_more_fault: 22.5 pts (police_report_field (hard_data) — DOC-007-CHP)
  doctrine: hb_837_51_bar — §768.81(6); HB 837 (eff. 2023-03-24)
  doctrine: rear_end_rebuttable_presumption — Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert
  doctrine: joint_several_abolished — §768.81(3)
Net per party: insured_vanguard_driver=97.50%, insured_vanguard_owner=2.50%, claimant_dubois=0.00%

basis_evidence.quoted_span: Vehicle 1 (insured) driver admits inattention and failure to stop; Vehicle 2 (claimant) was stopped at a red signal when struck from behind.
Primary collision factor: Vehicle 1 (insured) — failure to maintain a safe following distance. No contributing factors attributed to Vehicle 2.
41-year-old restrained driver, rear-ended while stopped at a signal. Ambulatory at the scene, transported for evaluation.

Persistent cervical radiculopathy following MVC of 2026-05-22. Impression: C4-C5 disc herniation; surgical consult pending; conservative tx exhausted. Recommendation: Refer to spine surgery for operative evaluation.

change_conditions: If Birge-category rebuttal evidence lands (mechanical failure / sudden stop / lane-change-by-lead / illegal-stop), rear-driver anchor shifts down materially.
If excess-judgment likelihood becomes clearer (damages evidence + low-limits confirmed), Powell duty to initiate tender solidifies.
Demand on file — sufficient-evidence assessment is the gating next decision; new evidence may flip the §624.155(4) call.
